title: pimpinella tragium subsp. lithophila
intro: Pimpinella tragium subsp. lithophila, also known as Lithophila, is a perennial plant species belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). Traditionally, Lithophila has been used for its medicinal properties, including its diuretic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to alleviate symptoms of urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and other related health issues. The plant's seeds and roots are often used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, to harness its health benefits, including the reduction of pain and inflammation. By incorporating Lithophila into one's herbal routine, individuals may experience improved urinary function and overall well-being.
